text.heading: Yar’Adua should go on vacation
text.p: SIR: The Alliance for Democracy (AD) has been following with rapt interest the dust raised by the call for the resignation of President Umaru Yar’Adua occasioned by his incessant ill health. Of particular interest to us is the propriety of Mr. President’s resignation and the attempt by the PDP to equate its interest with that of the generality of the over 150 million Nigerian people.

While we agree that Mr. President just like any human being can fall sick any time, but as the number one citizen of Nigeria upon whose shoulders the security and the welfare of the citizens of this country squarely rested, it therefore means that his state of health is the concern of every citizen of this country. By implication it is the duty of the Nigerian state to take care of him and it is equally the rights of the citizenry to know how much of the nation’s resources are being expended on him in this regard.

It is very clear to all, except the ruling PDP who are playing politics with Mr. President’s health that his health condition has gravely affected his ability to discharge his duties as the president of the country.

Rather than joining issues with those who are advocating for his resignation, we urge him to formally write the National Assembly concerning the true state of his health and apply for a leave of absence to allow him devote enough time to his wellbeing while his deputy takes over in the acting capacity till when he has fully recovered.

This would have been in accordance with S145 of the 1999 Constitution which states that ‘whenever the President remit to the President of the Senate and the Speaker of the House of Representatives, a written declaration that he is proceeding on vacation or that he is otherwise unable to discharge the functions of his office, until he transmit to them a written declaration to the contrary such functions shall be discharged by the Vice President as acting President’. This step is crucial for Mr. President to take in order to bring about stability in the system without also jeopardizing himself and his family.

p.italics: National Publicity Secretary, Alliance for Democracy
